title: ARPA-H Launches $144M PROSPR Program to Fund First Phase 3 Aging Trials
description: ARPA-H awarded $144M to seven teams to lay groundwork for phase 3 trials of aging interventions, including rapamycin, semaglutide, and SGLT2 inhibitors in healthy adults.

title: Daily Multivitamins Slow Biological Aging by 4 Months in Two-Year Trial
description: COSMOS trial data published in Nature Medicine show daily multivitamin slowed epigenetic aging across five clocks, with greatest benefit for those biologically older at baseline.

title: Rhonda Patrick Introduces 'Peakspan' Concept -- Aging is Asynchronous by System
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick shared a new paper proposing 'Peakspan' as a framework for how long you maintain 90% of peak function, noting each body system ages on its own timeline.

title: Naked Mole Rat Longevity Gene Successfully Transferred to Mice
description: Mice engineered with the naked mole rat hyaluronan synthase 2 gene showed a 4.4% increase in median lifespan, reduced cancer, and lower inflammation.

title: TRIIM-X Trial Shows 2.82-Year Epigenetic Age Reversal with Thymus Protocol
description: TRIIM-X results show combination of growth hormone, DHEA, metformin, zinc, and vitamin D3 reversed epigenetic age by 2.82 years on the Horvath clock in 65 participants.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches VITAL-H Trial -- First Phase 3 Aging Trial in Healthy Adults
description: Backed by $38M from ARPA-H, the VITAL-H trial will test rapamycin, dapagliflozin, and semaglutide in healthy adults aged 60-65 to delay age-related decline.

title: Rapamycin Protects Against Age-Triggering DNA Damage in New Oxford Study
description: University of Oxford study in Aging Cell shows low-dose rapamycin reduces T cell DNA damage and senescence markers in older adults by inhibiting mTOR pathway.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising 2026: $13.1B Across 93 Companies
description: Comprehensive ranking shows Altos Labs ($3B), Oura ($1.2B), and Retro Biosciences ($1.2B) lead longevity funding. Epigenetic reprogramming is the best-funded modality at $3.3B.

title: Brown University Wins $22M to Test Anti-Aging Drug Censavudine in Humans
description: ARPA-H-funded project will test HIV drug Censavudine to reduce chronic inflammation and slow aging in 200+ healthy adults aged 60-65 over 48 weeks.

title: Naked Mole Rat cGAS Gene Mutations Explain Exceptional DNA Repair and Longevity
description: Chinese researchers identified four amino acid changes in cGAS enzyme that enhance DNA repair in naked mole rats. Engineered fruit flies with these mutations lived longer.

title: Longevity Anti-Senescence Therapy Market to Reach $19.3B by 2030
source: National Law Review / Business Research Co.
description: Market growing at 6% CAGR driven by multi-drug combinations (metformin + rapamycin), expanding longevity clinics, and organ-on-a-chip aging research models.

title: Polyamines: Anti-Aging Compound May Also Fuel Cancer via Dual eIF5A Isoforms
description: Study reveals polyamines boost healthy cells via eIF5A1 but can turbocharge cancer through eIF5A2, highlighting context-dependent effects of longevity supplements.

title: Sauna After Exercise Boosts VO2 Max 30% More Than Training Alone
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick shared research showing 15 minutes of sauna after cycling improved aerobic fitness by ~3 ml/kg/min vs exercise alone over 8 weeks.

title: Multivitamin Slows Epigenetic Aging -- Cocoa Flavanols Show No Effect
description: Rhonda Patrick highlighted COSMOS trial results: daily multivitamin reduced epigenetic aging pace by 0.1-0.2 years vs placebo, while cocoa flavanol showed no effect.

title: Eric Verdin (Buck Institute): The Future of Research on Aging is Now
description: Buck Institute CEO discusses CD38 inhibitors that increase mouse lifespan by correcting NAD+ decline, and the broader landscape of aging intervention targets.

title: Next-Gen Senotherapeutics: AI/ML-Driven Strategies for Aging Interventions
description: Review article covers AI-driven discovery of novel senotherapeutics using advanced computational techniques to identify and target senescent cells.

title: AFAR Webinar: Unpacking Biohacking for Longevity (March 12)
description: American Federation for Aging Research hosts free webinar featuring UCLA's Thomas Rando, moderated by BOLD Longevity Fund's Sergey Young.

title: Lifespan.io Rejuvenation Roundup: Senolytic Effectiveness, NK Cell Restoration, FDA Clears First Reprogramming Trial
description: February 2026 roundup covers mitochondrial stress boosting senolytic effectiveness, Life Biosciences' FDA-cleared cellular reprogramming trial, and NK cell rejuvenation.

title: Biomarkers of Aging: Moving from Observation to Intervention Science
description: Growing discussion on LinkedIn about aging biomarkers enabling real-time tracking of interventions, shifting longevity from observation to measurable intervention science.

title: Longevity Market Report 2026-2036: AI, Genomics, and Regenerative Medicine Drive Growth
description: Market shifting from wellness to institutional healthcare with longitudinal health data as competitive assets. Payer participation and regulatory clarity identified as key enablers.

title: Exercise Outweighs Sleep for Lifespan: Short Sleepers Who Exercise Live Longer
description: Brady Holmer's analysis shared by Rhonda Patrick shows more exercise yields greater healthspan gains at any sleep level; short sleepers who exercise outlive normal sleepers who don't.

title: Loyal Raises $100M Series C for Longevity Drugs for Dogs
description: Dog longevity startup Loyal raised $100M Series C led by age1 and Baillie Gifford, bringing total funding to $205M for canine lifespan extension therapies.

title: NOVOS Receives Strategic Investment from Unilever Ventures
description: Longevity nutraceuticals company NOVOS secured strategic minority investment from Unilever Ventures, signaling big consumer interest in science-backed anti-aging supplements.

title: Aeovian Pharmaceuticals Raises $55M for Selective mTORC1 Small Molecules
description: Aeovian raised $55M Series B for next-gen selective mTORC1 inhibitors that could offer rapamycin-like benefits with fewer side effects, backed by Luma Group and Foresite Capital.

title: Advanced Antiaging Therapies Review: What to Expect for 2026
description: Comprehensive review covering MSC preparations, rapamycin PEARL trial results, and emerging antiaging candidates. Notes PEARL trial rapamycin had only 1/3 bioavailability of commercial form.
